Summary: The company plans to invest US$1 billion in reducing carbon emissions and has already raised US$500 million through sustainability-linked bonds.
Text: In our pursuit of reduced carbon emissions, we plan to invest US$1 billion across our operations. We became the first steel company globally to raise US$500 million through US dollar-denominated Sustainability Linked Bonds.

Summary: The company acknowledges climate change, committed to reducing emissions, and published a Climate Action Report aligning with TCFD recommendations.
Text: We acknowledge the global challenge of climate change and have committed to reducing our emissions to contribute to its mitigation. In March 2021, we pledged to adopt and uphold the recommendations of the TCFD. By aligning with these recommendations across its four pillars, we aim to enhance our understanding of climate change impacts and make informed decisions on current and future decarbonisation strategies. We released our first Climate Action Report. The key aim of this Report is to present in an engaging and informative manner our challenging decarbonisation strategy and the actions into which that strategy translates. This Report demonstrates our alignment to recommendations of the TCFD and provides stakeholders with: ‘clear, comprehensive, high-quality information on the impacts of climate change’. It addresses the four thematic areas of the TCFD: Governance, Strategy, Risk Management, and Metrics and Targets.

Summary: The company aims to reduce CO2 emissions intensity by 42% by 2030.

Text: We are strengthening our mission of attaining net neutrality in carbon emissions for all operations in our direct control.

Summary: The company emphasizes resource efficiency, carbon footprint reduction, social responsibility, and transparent corporate governance practices.
Text: Resource efficiency and carbon footprint ∞Social responsibility and impact investments ∞Employee engagement and retention metrics ∞Corporate governance and risk management ∞Financial transparency and shareholder communication

Summary: The company invests in research and development to reduce carbon emissions and develop sustainable products.
Text: JSW’s R&D efforts focus on reducing carbon emissions through innovations in steelmaking processes, enhancing energy and resource efficiency, and developing sustainable product alternatives like high-strength and low-weight steels for green mobility and infrastructure.

Summary: The company transferred a unit to focus on green steel production and reduce CO2 emissions.
Text: To support its strategy of manufacturing low-carbon steel for export markets and adopting green technologies such as hydrogen-based DRI, JSW Steel transferred its Salav unit, comprising a 0.9 MTPA DRI plant and auxiliary units to JSW Green Steel Limited. This move enables separate tracking of CO2 emissions and aligns with the Company’s long-term sustainability goals.

Summary: The company incorporates climate change targets into management incentives.
Text: The achievement of the targets towards climate change forms a part of the climate related management incentives for the relevant business unit managers and employees of our operations.

Summary: The company has established Climate Action Group (CAG) and Sustainability Action Group (SAG) to monitor climate change and mitigation efforts.
Text: To stay up-to-date with rapid developments related to climate change and monitor the progress of the mitigation efforts, the Company has constituted Climate Action Group (CAG) and Sustainability Action Group (SAG).

Summary: The Climate Action Group (CAG) and Sustainability Action Group (SAG) work together to develop and implement climate change mitigation strategies.
Text: The CAG and SAG, facilitated by the corporate sustainability team, operates as a central think-tank to formulate and drive our climate change mitigation strategy and actions towards a low-carbon future.

Summary: The company's operations are guided by policies that focus on sustainability.
Text: All our operations are guided by policies that focus on key areas of sustainability, including environmental protection, social responsibility, and economic viability.

Summary: JSW Steel is progressively increasing the use of scrap in its manufacturing processes.
Text: We are progressively increasing the utilisation of scrap in our manufacturing processes.

Summary: The company offers a product that helps customers reduce their Scope 3 emissions.
Text: GreenEdge, customers can reduce their Scope 3 emissions, supporting the achievement of their sustainability goals.

Summary: The company implements projects to reduce CO2 emissions within its value chain.
Text: JSW Steel reduces CO2 emissions by implementing additional reduction projects and ensuring that the emission reductions are real and have occurred within our value chain.

Summary: The company's CO2 reduction efforts are independently verified by third-party certification bodies.
Text: All CO2 reductions are independently verified by leading third-party certification bodies.

Summary: The company offers customers the option to purchase low-carbon steel with verified reduction certificates.
Text: JSW GreenEdge Steel offers flexible CO2 reductions, allowing customers to purchase low carbon emission steel with verified reduction certificates as per their requirements

Summary: The company is taking steps to reduce its carbon footprint through various initiatives.
Text: We are actively lowering our CO2 emission footprint through the adoption of energy-efficient practices, increased use of renewable energy, waste heat recovery, and carbon capture pilots while transitioning to low-carbon fuels and green hydrogen pathways.

Summary: The company is adopting circular economy principles to reduce resource consumption.
Text: From reducing freshwater consumption to recycling waste and by-products, we are rethinking resource use in line with circular economy principles.

Summary: JSW Steel has implemented a Just Transition Policy to ensure a fair and equitable transition to a low-carbon future.
Text: JSW Steel has adopted a formal Just Transition Policy, recognising that the path to a low-carbon future must be inclusive, equitable, and socially responsible.

Summary: The company has outlined its climate strategy and assessed related risks and opportunities in a Climate Action Report.
Text: In our Climate Action Report, we have outlined our climate strategy along with an assessment of related risks and opportunities.

Summary: The company has a dedicated team working on a roadmap to integrate digital solutions for sustainability goals.
Text: With a dedicated Digital for Sustainability team, we have developed a roadmap to integrate digital solutions that support our sustainability goals.

Summary: The company collaborates with farmers to enhance skills, productivity, and market access.
Text: Collaborate with farmers, enhancing skills, productivity and market access to promote sustainable incomes.

Summary: The company is implementing technologies like green hydrogen and CCUS to reduce emissions.
Text: We are progressively integrating disruptive technologies such as green hydrogen and carbon capture, utilisation and storage (CCUS), alongside increasing scrap usage.

Summary: The company aims to power its operations with renewable energy by 2030.
Text: A critical enabler of this journey is our ambition to power our entire operations through renewable energy by 2030.

Summary: The company consumed 78.66 kgs of CFC-11 equivalent ODS in FY 2024-25.
Text: In FY 2024-25, the total ODS consumed at our facilities amounted to 78.66 kgs of CFC-11 equivalent.

Summary: The company states its commitment to various regulatory and social compliance measures.
Text: Compliance with environmental regulations ∞Pollution control measures ∞Adherence to social welfare regulations ∞Corporate social responsibility initiatives ∞Regulatory compliance and reporting

Summary: The company aims to promote sustainable practices and advocate for environmental protection and worker welfare.
Text: Promotion of sustainable practices within the industry ∞Environmental advocacy ∞Setting standards for worker welfare

Summary: The company has committed to achieving carbon neutrality by 2050.
Text: The Company is committed to achieving carbon neutrality across all operations under its direct control by 2050.

Summary: The Steel Scrap Recycling Policy, 2019, aims to promote a circular economy and green transition by encouraging scrap recycling.
Text: Aimed at supporting a circular economy and green transition, the policy promotes scientific processing and recycling of ferrous scrap by encouraging the establishment of scrapping centres.

Summary: JSW Steel is committed to achieving net-zero carbon emissions by 2050 through a decarbonization agenda.
Text: Our response At JSW Steel, we are committed to achieving Net Neutral Carbon Emissions by 2050 through a two-phase decarbonisation agenda.

Summary: JSW Steel received a high ESG score in the S&P Global Corporate Sustainability Assessment, ranking second globally in the steel sector.
Text: We had participated in the 2024 S&P Global Corporate Sustainability Assessment conducted to construct the Dow Jones Sustainability Indices and received an S&P Global ESG Score of 82, which puts us in the 99th percentile, securing the 2nd position globally in the steel sector.

Summary: JSW Steel's Just Transition framework aims to distribute the benefits of the green transition while minimizing risks for stakeholders.
Text: Our Just Transition framework is designed to ensure that the benefits of the green transition are shared widely, while risks are mitigated with empathy and foresight.

Summary: JSW Steel's Just Transition Policy is based on principles of sustainable development, decent work, social inclusion, and human rights.
Text: The policy is grounded in core principles of sustainable development, decent work, social inclusion, and respect for human rights.

Summary: The company aims to achieve net neutrality of carbon emissions by 2050.
Text: ∞Targeting net neutrality of carbon emissions by 2050.

Summary: The company aims to achieve carbon neutrality at its coated operations by 2030.
Text: ∞Achieving carbon neutrality at coated operations by 2030.

Summary: The company aims to maintain zero liquid discharge.
Text: ∞Maintain zero liquid discharge.

Summary: JSW Steel has been recognized for its sustainability performance by being included in the DJSI World and Emerging Markets Indices.
Text: JSW Steel has been included in the DJSI World Index, as well as the DJSI Index for Emerging Markets, for the second consecutive year.

Summary: The company states its policies are based on various international standards and frameworks, including the UN Sustainable Development Goals.
Text: The policies are based on NGRBC taking account of the requirements of various international standards like ISO 9000, ISO 14000, ISO45001, ISO 50001, ISO26000, SA8000, IFC Performance Standards, OECD Guidelines, UNGC guidelines and ILO Principles, ILO Convention on Human Rights, Report on Affirmative Action by CII, National Action Plan on Climate Change, National Environmental Policy, UN Sustainable Development Goals, Global Reporting Initiative, Carbon Disclosure Project (CDP), Dow Jones Sustainability Index (DJSI) and Task Force on Climate- related Financial Disclosures (TCFD) and TNFD.

Section: SECTION B – MANAGEMENT AND PROCESS DISCLOSURES
Summary: The company describes its corporate governance framework, which includes a board of directors with committees dedicated to business responsibility, sustainability reporting, risk management, and stakeholder relationships.
Text: The Company has a Corporate Governance Framework in place. There is a balanced Board of Directors comprising Executive and Non-Executive Independent Directors with diverse range of experience and expertise. The Board of Directors – 1. Provides strategic direction and evaluates overall performance 2. Ensure the long-term interest of the stakeholders are being served There are several Board Committees and the main ones are – 1. Business responsibility/sustainability reporting 2. Risk Management 3. Corporate social responsibility 4. Stakeholder Relationship 5. Audit 6. Project Review 7. Nomination & Remuneration etc

Summary: The company states its initiatives aim to provide sustainable water access and improve community health.
Text: these initiatives ensure sustainable water access and healthier futures for our communities amidst growing water stress and environmental challenges.

Summary: The company emphasizes its role in creating employment opportunities.
Text: As we grow further at an unprecedented speed, we continue to create enormous direct and indirect employment opportunities for a young nation, aspiring to be the world’s manufacturing hub.
